posts.author: battousai
posts.body: @D1D4K10 DDP is a lot more dynamic and we'll rounded on the ground than Strickland yet he had absolutely nothing to offer Khamzat. His entire strategy once he realized Khamzat was too strong was to just lose rounds, stay safe and hope for an opportunity for a hail mary finish once Khamzat tired himself out. And honestly, it almost worked. Strickland is competent enough on the ground to stay safe once he ends up there, but I dont know if he has the patience to fight Khamzat the way DDP did. I think Strickland gets subbed trying to make his way back to his feet. The only arguement I can see for Strickland is that his counter wrestling is a lot better than DDP. DDP doesn't mind a fight hitting the mat. Strickland wants to stay on his feet at all costs if he can help it. But lets be serious, even if his counter wrestling and TDD is on point for this fight, hes probably still gonna be on his back at some point in every round. We've already seen Strickland vs. DDP fight for almost an entire hour. They seem pretty evenly matched if you asked me. I just dont see what Strickland is really offering Khamzat in terms of a threat, especially after Khamzats displayed pretty good cardio in his last fight. Thats the one thing Strickland can weaponize against his opponents and its not gonna work here. Khamzats last performance may have been boring as hell but it was still a complete and utter domination of someone who's already beaten Strickland twice.

posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.body: Predictions: 12 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 3196 others Chimaev vs. StricklandSean Strickland, TKO, R4Here the ****** thing you guys.... Strickland doesnt lose to guys that ******** to cartoons or terrorists! We got a motivated Strickland back in a title fight and honestly I think theres a good chance he can pull this off. Khamzat is an emotional fighter with a fragile ego. If Sean can get in his head in the build up I really feel like it could cause him to want to prove his skills on the feet and thrown down like he did against Gilbert Burns. If that happens I think Sean can honestly piece him up and finish him and even if he does wrestle I think Sean can work his way back up to his feet as long as he's careful about not getting caught in a sub.Volkov vs. Cortes-AcostaWaldo Cortes-Acosta, DECI see a lot of people on Volkov here but I think Waldo has a style that could present problems to Volkov. Volkov is big, powerful, technically sound, and good at distance management. But he is SLOWWWWW as hell. His movements are slow, his punches are slow, and he's gonna be too slow to get out of the way of Waldos high volume and snappy straight punches. I could see a world where Volkov actually looks to grapple here but I dont have a lot of faith in his takedown offense. I think Salsa Boy cashes as an underdog like he so often does.Brady vs. BuckleySean Brady, DECThis one is insanely hard to pick not because its a 50/50 but because you just know that whoever loses is getting the doors blown off of them. Im gonna lean Brady here just cause he's more consistent. Sure he's been chinned a couple times but he bounced back pretty well after losing to Belal so I think mentally he's going to be okay getting back in to the cage. He's pretty ****** though if he cant get the takedown. His striking leaves a lot to be desired but again, he only looks bad in these fights where the takedown isnt as big of a threat like with Belal and Morales who are elite wrestlers. Buckley isnt going to put up that kind of resistance which is why I think he gets wrestle ****** and mopped here.Green vs. StephensKing Green, TKO, R2Should be a pretty wild scrap but like.... Why? Why is a 39 year old 145er with 22 losses getting a shot at King Green where there are so many 155ers with name value that could be getting this opportunity?Błachowicz vs. GuskovBogdan Guskov, TKO, R2Another fight where Im just going... why? Both guys could have moved on to new opponents after that last fight but here we are again running it back. I picked Jan last time but I think Im going Bogdan KO here. He had Jan hurt pretty badly in their last fight and if he didnt get caught himself at the very end of the fight I think he would've won that decision. Jan is just looking old and stiff for my liking and even though he's probably the most badass 43 year old on the planet right now he's well past his prime and hasnt won a fight in ages.Alvarez vs. AmosovYaroslav Amosov, SUB, R1Im gonna make the possible mistake of picking this fight based on each guy's last performance. Amosov looked like a beast and he was able to work his grappling on Magny with ease which is actually kind of impressive. Alvarez on the other hand didnt look the same to me at welterweight even with a gimme matchup. Just felt like he looked a bit too skinny and like his power didnt carry over up a weight class which both could be a problem in this one. Give me Youre-A-Slob Im-A-Slob via slobby blobby sub.

posts.author: A_Person_05
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 3196 others Chimaev vs. StricklandSean Strickland, DECHonestly I'm picking Strickland more because I want him to win than actually thinking he can win. He has much better striking and cardio than Khamzat, he is a much more well rounded fighter, much more tested. Everything I just said is true about all 3 of Khamzat's last opponents. Khamzat still wins. I just want Strickland to win so the Middleweight division isn't stuck with one title fight every 1.5 years, until the Champ vacates to go to LHW after 2 title defenses.Volkov vs. Cortes-AcostaAlexander Volkov, DECVolkov via reach, skill, and athleticism. Volkov just pieces him up, similar to the Pavlovich fight for both guys.Brady vs. BuckleySean Brady, DECBrady can do exactly what Usman did, maybe even better given where Usman is at in his career. If Buckley wins this by being this insanely physical striker jumping around in out, laterally moving landing big shots, using his insane frame and strength to prevent takedowns, I am going to be so pissed. Because Buckley straight up looked like he was not trying against Usman, if he won that he's getting a title shot rn. And the way Buckley beats Brady if it happens, is the same way he was supposed to beat Usman.Green vs. StephensKing Green, DECWhat a fun matchup of the uncs. I think Green is so much faster at where these guys both are, I think he gets it done by decision, using that speed, reach, weird frame. This will be an amazing boxing fight though.Błachowicz vs. GuskovJan Błachowicz, DECI picked Jan the first time I'm gonna do it again. He is so savvy. He can win this in more ways. Grappling, kickboxing, leg kick spamming, he has better defense than Guskov, he is more well rounded than Guskov. Guskov just has stupid nuclear power in his hands. He hits people and they look like they don't know what hit them. Guskov hurt Jan worse than Pereira ever did. I just have to pick the dude with more ways to win.Gautier vs. DiazAteba Gautier, TKO, R1Gautier murders a can, gets given another can next. Guy will break Anderson's winstreak record before he fights a top 5 opponent. In all seriousness, Gautier will pressure Diaz, Diaz is gonna land some BS like jabs and tappy leg kicks, Gautier will throw some monster combinaion, Diaz is gonna look shocked by the power of the shots because that's what happens when Gautier hits people, and then Gautier is gonna grab the head, and get a knockout with a knee.Kopylov vs. TulioRoman Kopylov, DECKopylov works the body wins a decision. Tulio pressuring Kopylov isn't gonna work out well for him IMO.Carpenter vs. OchoaJose Ochoa, TKO, R2Ochoa is damn good IMO. The Almabaev fight was a bad matchup on short notice. He looked damn good against Kavanaugh, hurt Loneer worse than anyone other than Charles Johnson ever has. He looked sick against Durden. He has a big frame, he has big power, he has nasty BJJ, and he has really nice movement skills.Susurkaev vs. SantosBaisangur Susurkaev, TKO, R1Susurkaev is probably the worst undefeated fighter on the roster rn, outside of random contender series NPCs that nobody cares about. So the worst relevant undefeated fighter. His boxing defense is some of the worst in not only that division, but maybe the entire UFC. He does not cover up, he does not move his head, he doesn't even tuck his chin. He is also a tiny middleweight. What he does have going for him is the fact that offensively he seems to have a massive variety of weapons, and has the fight iq to know how to set up those weapons really really well. He will chain together attacks to land big shots, he uses his footwork to land big shots, he knows how to land big leg kicks whenever his opponent is putting weight on their leg, and everything he lands has big power. I don't think Santos is the guy to beat him. He beat Ozzy Diaz, that was a robbery, he should be 2-0 in the UFC, but I think to beat Susurkaev you need to be a finisher. You need to capitalize on his terrible defense, and the fact that he seems small out there. Susurkaev can get Santos into a position to be finished, and Susurkaev is a finisher. If Susurkaev fought like Bruno Ferreira or Gregory Rodrigues, or Paulo Costa, Joe Pyfer, or Bo Nickal, every single one of those guys finish Susurkaev like it's nothing. Santos is not on that level.Alvarez vs. AmosovJoel Alvarez, TKO, R2Alvarez is a dog. Finisher from any position. Power in everything. Finished Luque don't let the result and the ref tell you otherwise, that was a TKO victory via a punch to the eye. Amosov is good but Alvarez is on another level. Losing to Arman doesn't mean anything, getting outgrappled by Arman doesn't mean anyone can outgrapple you, it means you got outgrappled by probably the third best grappler in the UFC.Dawson vs. RębeckiMateusz Rębecki, TKO, R2Dawson has no chin. He just cannot take a shot. Rebecki has good grappling, is a short guy so he can use that to help defend takedowns, and he just will land a big shot at some point, and Dawson cannot get hit by hard shots.Also let it be known that this is a ******* banger of a card. So many good fighters or prospects to look out for on this card. It does look like the main card might be a bit of a decision fest, but the prelims is gonna be 95% finishes. * Edited at 04.04.2026, 2:02 AM ET *

posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.body: Predictions: 12 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 3196 others Van vs. TairaJoshua Van, DECThe injury taking him out of the last fight is concerning but I'm a Josh Van stan through in through. I think we've seen Taira struggle more with strikers than we have seen Van struggle with grapplers and Im not gonna overthink it here. Yes Taira is bigger, yes he's more well rounded and yes he's been training MMA for longer but Im telling you I have a funny feeling Van stuffs the takedowns and pieces him up over the course of 5 rounds.Gautier vs. DiazAteba Gautier, TKO, R1Lol this is so stupid. Susarkaev vs Gautier was right there but I guess we need to see these guys both on 5 fight win streaks before ever facing anyone decent.Kopylov vs. TulioMarco Tulio, DECAnother well matched fight but Im taking Tulio to bounce back. Kopylov is a bit overrated and doesnt handle pressure fighters very well as we've seen in his last two fights. Tulio is a bit defensively irresponsible but I think he doesnt have to worry about a flash KO like he did last fight which will lead to a better performance.Carpenter vs. OchoaJose Ochoa, DECGood match making here. Both guys have a lot of potential but have fallen a bit short of the hype. I think I like Ochoa's upside a bit more so Im going to lean him to win here. Kind of a 50/50 though so if I can get Carpenter at decent underdog money I might take a swing.Susurkaev vs. SantosBaisangur Susurkaev, TKO, R2I hate this guy and hope he loses but I cant see Santos doing more than just taking a round off him early in the fight with volume. I think eventually the body shots from Susarkaev will make him crumble to the mat.Dawson vs. RębeckiMateusz Rębecki, TKO, R1Havent really been on the Rebecki side of things as of late but in this matchup I think hes got a really strong chance to get back in the win column. His striking and grappling are both really solid its just that the guy cant seem to avoid getting his face rearranged every time he's out there. Dawson has a similar problem with getting hit only difference is that when he gets hit clean he gets finished. I think because of that Rebecki has a good chance to put him away via TKO.Sabatini vs. GomisPat Sabatini, DECKind of messed up that UFC is giving Sabatini a step down in competition but its not totally shocking. They arent going to reward guys with boring fighting styles and zero fan fair with big matchups and I totally agree with it. From what Ive seen this fight can be tricky considering Gomis is a good striker with really good scrambles and takedown defense against the cage. Only problem is Pat Sabatini is like a Merab where he'll just keep shooting takedowns and failing until he finally gets it. The only formula to beat him that we've seen so far is to put his lights out but Gomis isnt a very powerful guy so I think this will be a victory grinded out by Pat in the later rounds.

posts.author: JeanSilvaEnjoyer
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 3197 others Chimaev vs. StricklandChimaev, DECNo one stops Chimaev from taking them down, and I don't think Sean will be any different. The main question I have is whether Chimaev will find a finish. I hope he is a little more aggressive here than he was against Dricus because that fight was a pain to watch, and Sean seems to be doing everything possible to make Chimaev extremely pissed off. Maybe that's part of the gameplan. Maybe he's trying to make Chimaev angry and more reckless. I don't think it'll make any difference though. Sean's a tough guy to KO and a tough guy to submit, so I'll predict he makes it to the final bell.Van vs. TairaTaira, DECVan is clearly the superior striker here, and Taira is clearly the superior grappler. I like both guys, and I'd like to see Van stay champ for a while, but I'm a little worried for the guy. For one thing, he's coming off some sort of minor injury that caused them to push the fight back a couple of weeks. It might mean nothing, or it might mean a week or two where he could not train. The best evidence we have for Van's grappling defense is the Tsuruya fight. And while Tsuruya's decent and Van did a good job of staying on his feet, Tsuruya is nowhere near Taira's level. Not even close. Of course, that was over a year ago, and Van continuously shows that he is growing at a rapid pace. But Taira is young, too, and I'm not so sure who's growing more in the shorter period of time. Van hits hard. He's KO'd Bunes and Silva, and he was damn close to KO'ing Durden and Royval, all of which he managed to do in 3 rounds. The thing that pushes me away from picking him is how Tsuruya's takedown attempts kept Van from putting out the same volume that he normally does. While Van still won that fight 30-27, Tsuruya really wasn't able to rack up any meaningful amount of control time, and I think Taira will. He could find a submission, but Van's only ever been subbed once in his early career, so I think that's unlikely. I find it more likely that we go to a 48-47 decision where 50% of people call it a robbery afterward, regardless of who wins.Volkov vs. Cortes-AcostaVolkov, DECWaldo has been looking great recently and I'm really tempted to go with him here, but Volkov is a mainstay of this division. I'm a little concerned because Volkov is 37, and he didn't look great against Almeida even if he did technically win. The thing that gets me about Waldo is that yes, he's coming off three KO wins, but the quality of competition is a little suspicious. Gaziev is a decent HW despite the memes, but he's not ever going to be in title contention. I thought more of Delija at the time, but the loss to Spivac he took (a loss in a primarily striking-based fight, mind you) is making me reevaluate my position on Delija. And Derrick Lewis is done. He can either chin you in the first round of a fight, or else you win. I still can't get the memory of Waldo almost losing to Spivac out of my mind, or him smudging Despaigne to a decision. I might switch on this one, but I'm leaning Volkov just because I tend to doubt the guy before each fight, and then he seems to always make me wonder why I doubted him.Brady vs. BuckleyBuckley, TKO, R2100% biased pick, but idgaf. WE are one of the most entertaining contenders at 170, WE are going to slime out Sean Brady, and then WE are going to get slimed out by Michael Morales or Carlos Prates. I understand why Brady's the favorite here because Joaquin fought Usman like he had never seen a takedown before in his life. It was a terrible look and a humiliating loss. I didn't know this before the Usman fight, but one of the announcers said that Buckley doesn't train with the best competition in the gym, and after looking at the page for "Finney's HIT Squad," yeah, that seems to be the case. But Buckley's been talking big on social media about how he's becoming "Buckgomedov" and he's never going to be held down by another man again. Of course, that's all talk, and who knows how much work he's actually doing on takedown defense in the gym. It's so easy to imagine Brady doing the exact same thing that Usman did to Buckley (and maybe even finding the sub), but I'm skeptical. Most of Brady's fight with Burns played out with striking in the center minus a couple of takedowns and a lot of clinching against the cage. I think he's gotten just a bit lucky with his matchups given that one of his last five was against Burns on his way out the door, and two of the others were against Edwards and Gastelum, who I don't think take their careers seriously anymore. Now, the exact same criticism could be made of Buckley because he beat ultra-washed Colby Covington and 41 year old Wonderboy. But I think Buckley is dangerous on the feet, Brady's chin is not the best, and I'm hoping that Buckley's not lying about training to defend those takedowns.Green vs. StephensGreen, DECI'm thinking it's probably not a finish because Stephens is generally a pretty durable guy. I mean, he just went five rounds in BKFC with Mike Perry before getting finished, and that means he can take a punch. But Bobby Green has looked good recently, and I don't think Stephens is ready for UFC competition again.Gautier vs. DiazGautier, TKO, R1I mean, I like watching Ateba knock these guys out as much as the next guy, but this matchmaking by the UFC is really slimy. They'll give PFL guys someone ranked in their first or second bout, but they're protecting Ateba like he's a precious jewel.Alvarez vs. AmosovAmosov, DECI've got a sneaking suspicion that Alvarez did well at 155 because of his physical frame and because he could out grapple most of his opponents. Those things are both gone for him here. Although Alvarez handled business against Luque, it wasn't quite the statement win that I expected. I'm still not sold on this guy at 170, but maybe I'm an idiot. Amosov faced a pretty strong level of competition in Bellator, but at the end of the day, it's still Bellator.Dawson vs. RebeckiRebecki, TKO, R2Round-betski has been through a lot of wars and has built up a lot of scar tissue, so if he gets flat backed by Dawson and starts eating elbows, I expect to see a finish similar to the one Dawson got over Rafa Garcia. But I feel like I haven't seen Rebecki on his back a whole lot in the UFC. The only times I remember were against CDF after he was already badly hurt and against Orolbai. He was able to withstand Orolbai's grappling quite well considering what we've seen Orolbai do to guys like Musayev and Chris Curtis, but maybe that was because he and Orolbai were covered in blood for most of that fight. I think Dawson is going to have a lot of trouble getting Rebecki to the mat. Rebecki's built like a bulldog. And even though we've seen Rebecki outworked on the feet by CDF, Ludovit Klein, and Chris Duncan, I don't think Dawson's on that level in terms of his striking. One thing that gives me concern is that Dawson is super close with Chris Duncan, so Duncan will have plenty of pointers to give to his teammate going into this bout. Either way, Rebecki makes everyone who fights him go through hell to win, and my early lean is that Dawson won't get his takedowns and will not be able to hang with Rebecki on the feet.Miller vs. GordonGordon, DECJared Gordon nearly beat Rafa Garcia with one leg after a car crash. In his prime, I'd pick Jim Miller to beat Gordon, but this ain't the Golden Era anymore.Kopylov vs. TulioTulio, DECNot really sure on this one. Kopylov's not looked great recently, but he's lost against guys who Tulio would also lose to. Meanwhile, Tulio got absolutely demolished by CLD in a way that I find it hard to believe Kopylov would get demolished. Might switch up on this one. It's mostly vibes.Sabatini vs. GomisSabatini, DECI think Chepe Mariscal is a great fighter, and I remember him cutting Sabatini open badly early in their fight. That's a nightmare for Sabatini especially because the blood is an extra obstacle to his grappling, but he got that fight where he wanted it and secured the W. Gomis, meanwhile, is also a talented dude, but I don't like how he makes everything so, so close. Split decisions with Marshall. Split decision with Brito. Close decision with Ruchala. Sabatini is kind of past his prime here, but I reckon he wins. Probably by split decision.Susurkaev vs. SantosSusurkaev, TKO, R3Low-key a closer fight than the pick percentage would have you believe. Maybe I'm wrong about Susurkaev, but he seems a little over hyped just because he's Khamzat's training partner. He's looked pretty good in the UFC so far, and I don't think he's a scrub by any means, but if he wants to be a real contender at 185, he shouldn't be losing rounds to Eric Nolan and Eric McConico. He plays around and gets much too ****y in the cage. Even though Djorden Santos is a goofy ahh fighter who will almost certainly lose here, he does not give up, and I expect him to give Susurkaev problems early.Carpenter vs. OchoaOchoa, TKO, R2The UFC is arguably giving Clayton his toughest fight yet even though he's on a two-fight losing streak. I think Ochoa is destined for the top 10. He got Lone'er Kavanagh on his debut and took Kavanagh to a super close decision. He blew through the GOAT Cody "Custom Made" Durden, and then he did pretty well pulling up on super-short notice against Asu Almabayev, who is a stylistic nightmare for Ochoa. I don't think Carpenter is going to be able to keep Ochoa down, and I think he's going to get hurt badly on the feet. Decision's probably the safer pick, but I'm going with my faith in big Jose here. * Edited at 05.02.2026, 5:52 PM ET *

posts.author: MrIzdik
posts.body: Predictions: 11 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 3197 others It is interesting that Strickland hasn't faced a single submission attempt in 24 UFC fights. I remember people raving about Sean's BJJ since the Tom Breese fight. So maybe he is just that sound defensively that he has avoided any submission attempts. But before fighting Khamzat, Whittaker had only faced one submission attempt and then we watched Khamzat break his face with a crank. I almost feel like we are just trying to predict what Khamzat wants to do here. Does he just want to take him down and land 500+ strikes like he did against DDP? Or does he want to run through him like he did Rob? I can almost see Sean's spite making him fight so defensively that he just avoids the finish. But something is telling me Khamzat wants to make a statement. This is a bit of a dollar store Conor/Khabib situation. I think if you poke the Russian/Chechen bear, then you are going to get mauled. So I think I am gonna go Khamzat sub.
